name: Mississippi Mud Bars
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F.
2. Line a 9 by 13 inch baking pan with aluminum foil, extending the end of the foil 1 inch over the sides of the pan. Grease and flour the foil, then set aside.
3. In a medium saucepan, melt 1 cup butter. Stir in 1 1/2 cups sugar and 1/2 cup cocoa powder. Remove from heat and beat in eggs one at a time until blended. Stir in flour and pecans until well combined. Pour into prepared pan.
4. Bake for 25 minutes or until a cake tester inserted into the center comes out clean. Let cool in the pan on a wire rack.
5. For the frosting, combine confectioners' sugar and cocoa powder in a medium bowl. Beat in melted butter and vanilla until smooth. Add enough milk (1 to 2 tablespoons) until fluffy and spreadable.
6. Using the ends of foil, lift the brownie layer from the pan and spread the frosting evenly over top. Sprinkle with 1/2 cup chopped pecans.
7. Cut into 2 by 1 inch bars and store in an airtight container.
